feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day, plus Polish public-holiday reminders

entry.title: The Uplift: The American Dream
entry.content: We celebrate the United States ahead of its 250th anniversary with stories about American heroes and pastimes. The founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics shares how he made a massive life change to become a Catholic priest.

entry.title: Strong earthquake hits Mexico-Guatemala border, triggering tsunami alert
entry.content: The U.S. Geological Survey reported that the earthquake had a magnitude of 7.3 with the epicenter 30 miles southwest of Aquiles Serdan, Mexico.

entry.title: Novak Djokovic talks tennis career, aging in the sport and Serena Williams' return
entry.content: Novak Djokovic talks to "CBS Mornings" about still competing at 39 years old, the U.S. Open and Serena Williams' return to the court.

entry.title: ICE officer in Maine shooting has history of violent behavior, relatives say
entry.content: The Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer who shot and killed a Colombian man in Maine this week is an Army veteran who has struggled with serious mental health issues since early childhood, relatives say.

entry.title: Netflix stock plunges to 52-week low following mixed earnings report
entry.content: Netflix stock declined 11% on Thursday morning, after investors raised concerns about Netflix's future growth following its second-quarter earnings report.

entry.title: Mullin pledges to go after voter fraud after Trump revives election interference claims
entry.content: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin spoke about election security a day after President Trump claimed during his primetime address that China interfered in the 2020 election. CBS News' Jake Rosen joins to discuss Mullin's remarks.

entry.title: Darline Graham in serious discussions about running for Lindsey Graham's seat
entry.content: Darline Graham indicated in a White House meeting Thursday that she's weighing a bid, CBS News confirmed.

entry.title: Boomer wealth transfer will mostly benefit the already rich, report finds
entry.content: Trillions of wealth passed down by the baby boomers will likely go to younger Americans who are already rich. Here's how much they'll get.

entry.title: Remarkable’s new Paper Pure is good. That’s why I wrote this review on it.
entry.content: Remarkable's Paper Pure replaces Remarkable 2, and it is quite good.

entry.title: Coca-Cola temporarily halts production of Fairlife milk after cyberattack
entry.content: A cybersecurity incident has forced Coca-Cola to suspend Fairlife milk production in the U.S. An investigation is underway.

entry.title: "Worst case scenario" for shippers as U.S. and Iran step up attacks
entry.content: As the U.S. and Iran escalate attacks 140 days into the war, an industry analyst says "nobody is willing to move" through the Strait of Hormuz.

entry.title: No product? No problem. This Disrupt 2026 session shows how to get pre-seed funding with conviction, storytelling
entry.content: It’s not just you: AI startups are taking in a huge amount of seed funding, and in the process making things harder for anyone looking for funding even at a pre-seed stage. We’ve covered the trend in detail, and at this year’s TechCrunch Disrupt event, we want to help pre-seed founders now being held to seed-stage […]

entry.title: Brenda Fricker, Oscar winner who played Pigeon Lady in "Home Alone 2," dies
entry.content: Brenda Fricker won an Academy Award for 1989's "My Left Foot," played the Pigeon Lady in "Home Alone 2" and appeared in "A Time to Kill" and "So I Married an Axe Murderer."

entry.title: ‘TODAY’ show intruder arraigned on hate crime charges
entry.content: The man who allegedly breached security and shouted a racial slur at “TODAY” co-anchor Craig Melvin was arraigned on hate crime charges Friday.

entry.title: Can creditors change their minds after agreeing to forgive a debt?
entry.content: Before you assume a forgiven debt is settled, there are certain things to understand about the process.

entry.title: Airlines say permanent daylight saving time won't be simple
entry.content: Adjusting to permanent daylight saving time would cause significant disruptions to schedules and operations, an airline trade group said.

entry.title: Zoox issues software recall after a robotaxi got confused by heavy smoke
entry.content: The recall comes as the top automotive safety regulator in the U.S. has warned AV companies about their vehicles interfering with first responders.

entry.title: Anne Hathaway and Matt Damon give inside look at "The Odyssey" and challenges during filming
entry.content: Anne Hathaway and Matt Damon, who star in "The Odyssey," speak to "CBS Mornings" about the movie, the challenges they faced during filming and working with Oscar-winning director Christopher Nolan.

entry.title: Appeals court keeps in place Pentagon's escort policy for reporters
entry.content: The appeals court in Washington, D.C., divided 2-1 in ruling in favor of the Trump administration in the legal battle over the Pentagon's escort policy for journalists.

entry.title: Help, my sofa is killing me! The toxic chemicals hiding in your home – and how to avoid them
entry.content: From mattresses to saucepans, scientists offer tweaks to help detoxify your spaceThe problem Whenever we burn anything inside our homes, we cause indoor air pollution, whether we’re cooking using gas, frying bacon, lighting a wood burner, an open fire, a cigarette or incense, or ruining the toast. “ We spend 80-90% of our time indoors,” says Prof Francis Pope, chair of atmospheric science at the University of Birmingham. “And there is potential to have quite high concentrations of pollution indoors. This affects your respiratory and cardiovascular systems; certain components are carcinogenic, and there’s a growing body of evidence that air pollution affects cognition. In the long term, you get awful diseases such as dementia and Alzheimer’s. But relatively short exposures to air pollution are linked to things like educational outcome, workplace productivity and general mental wellbeing.” Continue reading...

entry.title: When is hiring a debt relief company worth the cost?
entry.content: Pursuing debt relief can be a smart move but it isn't always worth paying for. Here's when it could pay off.

entry.title: Germany's fastest sprinter Owen Ansah faces an anti-doping process
entry.content: Germany's fastest-ever sprinter is facing a potential ban as the national anti-doping agency looks into whether he failed to comply with a test. Owen Ansah, the first German sprinter ever to run under 10 seconds for the 100 meters, remains free to compete while the German agency carries out what it called a “results management process.” The agency, known as NADA, said Friday that Ansah is being investigated under a rule which bars athletes from evading, refusing or failing to submit to doping tests.

entry.title: What is cyclosporiasis, the parasitic illness causing ‘explosive’ diarrhea?
entry.content: Illness surging in Michigan and other US states is rarely life-threatening, CDC says – but it can have severe effectsCDC investigates parasite that’s caused cases of ‘explosive’ diarrhea in 18 US statesCases of cyclosporiasis – a parasitic illness that can cause “explosive”, watery diarrhea – have surged across the United States in recent days, health officials have said, with an abnormally large outbreak of almost 1,000 cases reported in Michigan.Michigan typically reports about 50 cases a year, making the current outbreak the largest in the state’s history and one of the nation’s biggest in recent years. Ohio has also reported a sharp increase, with 177 cases as of 2 July, since the CDC’s last count. Continue reading...

entry.title: NASCAR reveals new rules for Cup Series superspeedway package in 2026
entry.content: NASCAR has revealed new rules for the Cup Series superspeedway package for the 2026 season, starting at Daytona International Speedway.

entry.title: Liam and Markus Ruck Are Living Their Hockey ‘Dream Come True’ After Being Drafted Together by Pittsburgh Penguins (Exclusive)
entry.content: The 18-year-old brothers were taken in the first and second rounds of the 2026 NHL Draft last month

entry.title: Bronze final? How about World Cup’s most pointless match? Why Fifa should bin off third-place play-off
entry.content: WORLD CUP 2026: Three days after the biggest loss of their careers, England’s squad have to go again and take on Kylian Mbappe’s France on Saturday in Miami. And Kieran Jackson argues: who even cares?

entry.title: 5 Eagles offensive players generating momentum ahead of training camp
entry.content: Tank Bigsby, Dontayvion Wicks and Eli Stowers are among five Eagles offensive players generating momentum ahead of training camp.

entry.title: What team did LeBron James go to? Here are the favorites
entry.content: LeBron James free agency odds shift as the Golden State Warriors tie the Cleveland Cavaliers for the top spot.

entry.title: Bucs' Tristan Wirfs ranked among best franchise pieces in the NFL
entry.content: Bleacher Report ranked Buccaneers left tackle Tristan Wirfs among the NFL's top franchise-building players.

entry.title: Here we go – Arsenal agrees deal for new winger
entry.content: According to Fabrizio Romano, Arsenal have agreed a deal to sign Christos Tzolis from Club Brugge as they begin to get serious about adding new players to their squad.The Gunners have experienced a re...

entry.title: Why the first GPU financiers are turning to inference chips in a $400 million deal
entry.content: A $400 million chip-backed loan points to the next wave of AI infrastructure deals.

entry.title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: Netflix, SpaceX, Alphabet and more
entry.content: These are the stocks posting the largest moves premarket.

entry.title: Taylor Farms Lettuce at Taco Bell Linked to Stomach Bug Outbreak
entry.content: The Food and Drug Administration is investigating a potential connection to iceberg lettuce supplied to Taco Bell by produce giant Taylor Farms as the possible source of the cyclosporiasis outbreak that has sickened thousands across 34 states. The fast-food giant is announcing a voluntary recall of some potentially impacted lettuce from its restaurants. NBC’s Anne Thompson reports for TODAY.

entry.title: Trump Uses Prime-Time Speech to Revive False Election Claims
entry.content: President Donald Trump used his prime-time speech Thursday to raise questions about election integrity in the build-up to November’s key midterms while reviving false claims about the 2020 election and asserting that China targeted his campaign. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

entry.title: Trump administration sues to stop Maryland from giving in-state tuition to illegal immigrants
entry.content: The Trump Justice Department has sued to end Maryland's law that allows illegal immigrants to get in-state tuition rates, saying it violates a federal law that limits states' ability to grant that benefit to those in the country illegally.

entry.title: The Boardroom Debate That Led to IBM’s Historic 25% Stock Plunge
entry.content: Plus, well-off boomers are upsizing, not downsizing—and a shortcut to private-equity riches is minting young millionaires.

entry.title: SEC Flooded With Complaints Over Plan to Scrap Required Quarterly Earnings
entry.content: The agency received more than 200,000 comments, a record number.

entry.title: This Shortcut to Private-Equity Riches Is Minting Young Millionaires
entry.content: Armed with grit and determination, these would-be business owners scrape together financing on a deal-by-deal basis to buy up HVAC outfits and specialized manufacturers.

entry.title: Gas Prices Will Stay Higher for Longer, Even if Oil Falls
entry.content: Refining capacity is strained, and fuel markets don’t have much of a buffer.

entry.title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq Futures Drop as Chip Selloff Deepens
entry.content: Netflix shares slide after the streamer warns of weaker growth

entry.title: Iranian bridges severely damaged after sixth night of U.S. strikes
entry.content: Bridges were severely damaged in Iran after the U.S. military said it completed its sixth consecutive night of strikes against the country. CENTCOM said in a statement posted on X that it targeted “coastal surveillance and air defense sites, military logistics infrastructure, and maritime capabilities.”

entry.title: ‘Forever chemicals’ – the national cancer scandal brewing in a Lancashire town
entry.content: In this week’s newsletter: residents living near a chemical factory are the latest community concerned about the health and environmental impact of Pfas contamination• Don’t get Down to Earth delivered to your inbox? Sign up hereResidents within a kilometre of the AGC Chemicals Europe factory in Thornton-Cleveleys in the north of England, have found themselves at the centre of what is quickly becoming a national scandal.In 2024, the UK Environment Agency and the local authority initiated an investigation into historic emissions of Pfoa – a carcinogenic “forever chemical” that international research has linked to kidney cancer – from the factory. After environmental testing, people have been advised to wash and peel homegrown food and to avoid eating locally produced eggs, and two allotment sites near the factory have been closed.May and June heatwaves killed about 2,700 people in England and Wales, data suggestsInside the secret Laos shops selling pangolin scales, bear bile and tiger bones to touristsWho is ‘stealing’ Bali’s water? How tourism siphoned off a prized resource Continue reading...

entry.title: Taking salsa classes can improve mental health, Oxford study suggests
entry.content: Researchers find dance reduced depressive symptoms and social anxiety in young people in eight-week trialSalsa is one of the most popular dance forms, with hundreds of millions of followers worldwide. A mix of Caribbean, Spanish and African musical styles and rhythms, it is believed to offer many cognitive and physical benefits, such as improving cardiovascular health, muscle tone and posture.Now a randomised controlled trial suggests it could also reduce depression and anxiety. Researchers at the University of Oxford and the Oxford Health NHS trust studied 121 young adults with mild to moderate depression and anxiety who were randomly assigned to a salsa dance programme or a control group. Continue reading...

entry.title: Commanders' 2026 uniform schedule disappointing for some
entry.content: For fans of the Commanders' new "Hail Raisers," you'll only get to see them twice in 2026.

entry.title: Roger Goodell has "about 10" more countries in mind for games, including Japan
entry.content: The intentional push continues.

entry.title: The Open 2026 leaderboard, Friday tee times, projected cut line, live updates
entry.content: Follow along with Golfweek for live leaderboard updates, highlights, breaking news and more from Friday's second round of the 2026 Open Championship.

entry.title: Insider reveals bad but expected news for Ohio State in its pursuit of an elite five-star weapon
entry.content: Ohio State is one of the finish for five-star WR Monshun Sales, but the latest around his recruitment signals he'll likely be picking another Big Ten school.

entry.title: “I Didn’t Really Want To Get Traded”: Jon Gruden Regrets Leaving Raiders for Buccaneers Despite Winning a Super Bowl
entry.content: Jon Gruden won a Super Bowl. He got the ring, the parade, the validation every coach chases.

entry.title: Natasha Cloud Urges NBA, WNBA Commissioners to Address Sports Betting Harassment
entry.content: Natasha Cloud urged WNBA commissioner Cathy Engelbert and NBA commissioner Adam Silver to do more to protect players from harassment tied to sports betting.

entry.title: Jude Bellingham shares his side of the viral confrontation that seemingly triggered Lionel Messi
entry.content: Jude Bellingham downplayed what appeared to be a serious confrontation with Lionel Messi during England’s 2-1 loss to Argentina in the World Cup semifinals. Bellingham and Messi were seen going at it during a stoppage of play in the first…

entry.title: Is Rohit Sharma set to retire at Lord's? Ex-India captain likely to play final game against England
entry.content: Reports emerge that the BCCI selection committee has moved past Rohit Sharma for the 2027 World Cup, making the 3rd ODI at Lord's his final match for India.

entry.title: NBA Commissioner wants LeBron James to pick his next team ASAP due to major unseen problem
entry.content: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver isn’t loving LeBron James’ extended decision-making process for his 2026 free agency. LeBron is going to switch teams for the fourth, and potentially, final time in his career this summer.

entry.title: Five things to know about Trump's election fraud allegations
entry.content: Five things to know about Trump's election fraud allegations

entry.title: LeBron James reveals his GOAT inspiration that is not Michael Jordan
entry.content: LeBron James is often considered by fans as the GOAT of basketball alongside Michael Jordan, but it seems Jordan is not the only inspiration he has.

entry.title: LeBron James reveals his GOAT inspiration that is not Michael Jordan
entry.content: LeBron James is often considered by fans as the GOAT of basketball alongside Michael Jordan, but it seems Jordan is not the only inspiration he has.

entry.title: Three Items ESPN Should Consider With Their Oversight Of The NFL Network This Season
entry.content: "Training camp has always been about evaluating talent, experimenting with ideas, and figuring out what gives you the best chance to win in the fall. ESPN should approach its first season overseeing NFL Network the same way." The post Three Items ESPN Should Consider With Their Oversight Of The NFL Network This Season appeared first on Barrett Media.

entry.title: The FIFA World Cup Experience Teaches Lessons American Sports Networks Can Learn From
entry.content: "The World Cup also reminded us that presentation matters. Every knockout match felt enormous because television treated it that way. The music. The entrances. The crowd. The anticipation. Nobody needs to convince viewers Spain versus Argentina matters. The broadcast simply gets out of the way and lets the occasion breathe." The post The FIFA World Cup Experience Teaches Lessons American Sports Networks Can Learn From appeared first on Barrett Media.

entry.title: Breaking down Red Wings' 2026-27 schedule: Another tough March awaits
entry.content: The NHL released its regular-season schedule for 2026-27 on Thursday, and it looks like the Red Wings could get off to another fast start.

entry.title: LeBron James shouts out Bronny, USC medical staff in TIME 100 speech
entry.content: LeBron James stresses the importance of health and cardiovascular devices which ultimately saved his son, Bronny, in 2023.

entry.title: Open Championship weather forecast: Will it rain in Round 2 today?
entry.content: Perfect weather is expected at Royal Birkdale for The Open Championship with clear skies and no rain forecast.

entry.title: India's biggest IPO this year rakes in bids worth $31 billion, powered by institutional frenzy
entry.content: The IPO of India's largest asset manager closed on Thursday, drawing keen interest from institutional investors.

entry.title: Nike adds eight College Football stars to NIL roster before 2026 season
entry.content: The top outfitter inks future NFL stars to deals.

entry.title: Pat Riley admits it was hard to part ways with multiple players in the blockbuster trade
entry.content: Pat Riley knows the Heat took a big risk by trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo, but it seems he does not regret it.

entry.title: The 'overlooked' Cameron Carr is motivated to prove the doubters wrong with the Lakers
entry.content: Cameron Carr has been making headlines for the Lakers at NBA Summer League, and he is ready to keep proving himself.

entry.title: All eyes are on Todd Monken as he takes command of Browns training camp
entry.content: A new era is officially underway in Cleveland. As Todd Monken leads his first training camp as the Browns’ head coach, all eyes will be on how the respected offensive mind shapes the team’s identity, develops its young talent, and begins laying the foundation for the franchise’s future.

entry.title: Special Report: Trump delivers speech on White House declassification of election documents
entry.content: Watch full coverage as President Donald Trump addresses the nation from the White House. Trump announced that the White House will declassify documents related to the 2020 election and alleged Chinese influence. NBC News' Hallie Jackson, Dan De Luce, and Kelly O'Donnell provide analysis of Trump’s remarks.

entry.title: Special Report: Trump delivers speech on election infrastructure
entry.content: NBC News correspondents break down President Trump's address to the nation on election integrity where the president described information he says is now declassified related to allegations about voter fraud, including accusations against China.

entry.title: How paper ballots make digital manipulation of results unlikely
entry.content: NBC News’ Jane Timm analyzes President Trump’s claims about digital manipulation of ballot results in the U.S., as Trump said that the White House will declassify documents related to the 2020 election and alleged influence by China during his speech to the nation.

entry.title: NBC News correspondents examine Trump statements on election integrity
entry.content: President Trump delivered an address to the nation where he announced the White House released declassified information in regards to election infrastructure. NBC News' correspondents Kelly O'Donnell, Ryan Nobles, and Dan De Luce examine some of Trump's comments during the speech.

entry.title: Trump administration to declassify documents relating to China and U.S. elections
entry.content: President Donald Trump addressed the nation from the White House, announcing that the administration will declassify documents related to the 2020 election and alleged influence by China.

entry.title: Watch Live: Trump speaks on elections, alleging Chinese access to voter data
entry.content: President Trump is giving a primetime address on elections tonight, as he remains focused on the 2020 race.

entry.title: Maine Democratic Senate candidates discuss which of Platner's ideas they would use
entry.content: Maine Democratic Senate candidates Nirav Shah, Shenna Bellows, Jordan Wood and Troy Jackson discuss which ideas from Graham Platner's platform they would use if they are chosen as the nominee.

entry.title: Dozens of Marineland's whales coming to U.S. through rescue plan
entry.content: Dozens of beluga whales are set to be relocated from the shuttered Canadian theme park to aquariums across the United States through an international emergency rescue effort, officials said.

entry.title: Watch: Wildfire smoke blankets cities across U.S., Canada
entry.content: Heavy smoke from wildfires in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing parts of the U.S., prompting air quality alerts in 18 states. These videos show the hazy conditions on the ground in several cities.

entry.title: FDA approves new cholesterol-lowering pill, doctor explains what it does differently
entry.content: The FDA has approved a new daily cholesterol pill that works differently from popular statins and may deliver better results for some people. Dr. Corey Bradley, a cardiologist at Columbia University's Irving Medical Center, joins "CBS Evening News" to discuss.

entry.title: New Mexico attorney general accuses Justice Department of hindering Epstein probe
entry.content: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche told senators at his confirmation hearing that the Justice Department will investigate any new leads related to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. New Mexico Attorney General Raúl Torrez accuses Blanche of stonewalling a live Epstein investigation right now, and he joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

entry.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak possibly traced to lettuce from Mexico used by Taco Bell
entry.content: A lettuce supplier to fast-food giant Taco Bell is being investigated as a possible source for a nationwide cyclosporiasis outbreak that has sickened thousands of people.

entry.title: Thousands of election-focused workers cut during Trump's 2nd term
entry.content: Since the beginning of his second administration, the government has cut thousands of workers who were tasked with ensuring secure elections in the U.S.

entry.title: Cyclosporiasis: officials looking at Taco Bell lettuce supplier as potential source of outbreak
entry.content: Taylor Farms reportedly linked to outbreak thought to be in Michigan, Ohio, West Virginia and KentuckyWhat is cyclosporiasis, the parasitic illness causing ‘explosive’ diarrhea?Amid an ongoing outbreak of cyclosporiasis, federal health officials are reportedly looking at iceberg lettuce from Taylor Farms that was supplied to Taco Bell as a possible source.On Thursday afternoon, the Washington Post, CNN and other media outlets reported that the lettuce supplier had been linked to the outbreak, citing unnamed sources “familiar with the investigation”. Continue reading...

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Some fortune cookies contain no fortune.
Lucky Numbers: 93, 43, 7, 20, 73, 14

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Have 3 Things to do in a Day – 1 Work task, 1 Personal Task and 1 tiny task.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: "Ithyphallophobia" is a morbid fear of seeing, thinking about or having an erect penis.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Confucius
entry.content: “Death and life have their determined appointments; riches and honors depend upon heaven.” — Confucius

entry.title: China’s Two-Speed Economy Pairs High-Tech Success With Domestic Gloom
entry.content: Why China’s soaring export numbers and tech prowess hide a brutal economy and a growing army of underemployed workers at home.

entry.title: Kris Jenner's mom, beloved matriarch Mary Jo 'MJ' Shannon, dies at 91
entry.content: Kris Jenner's mom, the beloved 'Keeping Up With the Kardashians' matriarch Mary Jo 'MJ' Shannon, died Thursday. She was 91.

entry.title: U.S. Targets Iranian Bridges in Bid to Choke Off Regime’s Supply Routes
entry.content: The strikes were aimed at Iranian sites used to launch attacks on commercial ships near the Strait of Hormuz.

entry.title: Midwest sees world's worst air quality as wildfire smoke spreads
entry.content: Smoke from Canadian wildfires is blanketing major cities across the U.S., obscuring iconic skylines and prompting air quality alerts. NBC News’ Maggie Vespa reports.

entry.title: Weather conditions again brought devastating floods to a vulnerable swath of Texas
entry.content: Severe storms have dumped over 20 inches of rain in parts of Texas, causing significant flooding

entry.title: Iceberg lettuce supplier being investigated by FDA as source of cyclosporiasis outbreak
entry.content: The Food and Drug Administration is investigating an iceberg lettuce supplier as the source of a parasite outbreak causing "explosive" diarrhea in people who contract the illness. Nationwide, nearly 7,000 people may have been sickened, with 1,645 of those cases confirmed and more than 5,100 still under investigation.

entry.title: Hegseth backs low-altitude military flyovers as a series of maneuvers draws scrutiny
entry.content: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is supporting low-altitude military flyovers as the maneuvers have faced scrutiny

entry.title: Hal Williams, 'Sanford and Son' and '227' actor, dies at 91
entry.content: Actor Hal Williams, who portrayed Officer Smitty on 'Sanford and Son' and Lester Jenkins on '227,' has died. He was 91.

entry.title: Netflix reports higher profits as investors worry about growth
entry.content: Netflix beat analyst expectations on net income in the second quarter, as the streamer sought to reassure investors that its business model remains strong.

entry.title: Wife of Colombian father killed by ICE in Maine says they had planned to grow old together
entry.content: Karolina Rojas wanted to make sure that her husband, Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero, was remembered the right way after he was fatally shot by an ICE officer in Biddeford, Maine.

entry.title: The Hollywood Bowl's beast of a new sound system is a work in progress
entry.content: The L.A. Phil began the first all-American week of the L.A. Phil Hollywood Bowl season with new works about soccer and Suffs led by Tito Munoz in his Bowl debut and Thomas Wilkins

entry.title: Saudi Arabia Was in the Midst of Transformation. Then Came the Iran War.
entry.content: A former ambassador on how the kingdom can protect its multibillion-dollar societal investment.

entry.title: Drugmaker Merck announces approval of new cholesterol-fighting drug
entry.content: Lipfendra can be taken in conjunction with or an alternative to statins, the most prescribed medication in the USSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The drug giant Merck on Thursday announced it had received FDA approval for a daily pill that can be an alternative to statins for treating high LDL levels, or so-called bad cholesterol. The new pill, Lipfendra, is a version of a drug called a PCSK9 inhibitor.For decades the go-to therapy to treat high cholesterol levels has been statins, the most prescribed medication in the US. But statins come occasionally with side-effects like muscle aches and they sometimes don’t drop cholesterol levels enough to meet recommended levels. Continue reading...

entry.title: Mississippi teachers say new state-mandated process for buying classroom supplies is more difficult
entry.content: Teachers in Mississippi say accessing state funds for classroom supplies has become more difficult with a new state-mandated process

entry.title: Trump administration caps how long international students can stay in the US
entry.content: The Trump administration has finalized a rule that will prevent international students from staying in the U.S. for more than four years unless they seek and obtain government approval, a departure from long-standing practice

entry.title: Ghost gun company ordered to pay $100M in death of Kentucky teen in historic verdict
entry.content: A ghost gun company has been ordered to pay over $100 million in the death of a Kentucky teenager who had purchased the company's pistol-building kit online

entry.title: NYC helicopter that crashed, killed 6 last year showed signs of a bird strike: NTSB
entry.content: The National Transportation Safety Board has reported that the remains of several geese were found on the wreckage of a sightseeing helicopter that crashed into the Hudson River last year in New York City and killed 6 people
